meta.auditor: Claude Code via Figma REST API + MCP + token diff + doc frame reader
evidence_sources: Figma REST API (variables/local, components, styles, file structure)

0.1_platform_architecture_clarity.narrative: MUI is an explicitly single-platform web system. The strategy is implicit rather than documented in the Figma file. Breakpoints, interaction states, and component APIs all target web. An agent can infer the platform but must rely on convention rather than declaration.

1.1_token_implementation.narrative: Colour tokens implemented as Figma Variables (palette: 136 vars, material/colors: 252 vars). Spacing tokens as Variables (14). Typography partially implemented (fontSize as Variables via aliases, other properties as text styles). Elevation as effect styles (24), not Variables.

1.2_alias_chain_integrity.narrative: Palette has 29 aliased variables forming semantic-to-primitive chains (e.g. primary/main aliases to blue/700). Local chains resolve without breaks. Spacing has no alias chains (flat values). Only colour has a complete chain.

1.3_token_architecture_depth.narrative: Primitive layer exists (material/colors). Semantic layer exists (palette). Component-level tokens partially exist (_components/alert/*, _components/paper/*). Non-colour categories (spacing, typography, shape) have no layered architecture. Spacing is flat (1=8, 2=16) with no semantic or component layers.

1.4_primitive_naming.narrative: Full words and slashes throughout (blue/700, not b-700). Consistent within collections. Namespace prefixes present. Spacing uses numeric names which is reasonable for a base-unit system.

1.5_token_format_machine_readability.narrative: Code-side tokens are programmatic (createTheme()). Extraction script produces normalised JSON. Format is parseable but requires the extraction step, not natively in DTCG or Style Dictionary format.

1.6_token_documentation.narrative: 72 of 446 local variables (16%) have descriptions. Descriptions present on palette state tokens and paper elevation tokens. Absent on material/colors primitives, spacing, typography, shape, and breakpoints. An agent cannot determine when to use most tokens from name and description alone.

2.1_component_to_token_binding.narrative: MCP spot-check of Alert shows fills bound to palette variables, strokes bound, border radius bound to shape variable, typography partially bound (family and weight via variables, size via _fontSize aliases). Spacing hardcoded in px throughout (16px, 6px, 12px, 7px, 4px). No comprehensive component-level token layer.

2.2_component_api_composability.narrative: Code-only dimension. Not assessed in this Figma-primary audit.

2.3_variant_completeness.narrative: 1034 published components. 826 with state variants. Button has 360 variants across 4 axes. Interactive states (Enabled, Hovered, Focused, Pressed, Disabled) consistently defined. Focus state on 171 components.

2.4_escape_hatch_usage.narrative: Code-only dimension. Not assessed in this Figma-primary audit.

3.1_component_description_coverage.narrative: 370 of 1034 components (35.8%) have any description. Of those, 356 are code import snippets. Functional intent detected in approximately 0% of descriptions. This confirms the v1.3 finding: descriptions are present but intent is absent.

3.2_documentation_structure_machine_read.narrative: Code-only dimension. Not assessed in this Figma-primary audit.

3.3_intent_quality.narrative: The 14 non-code-snippet descriptions are one-sentence summaries from the MUI docs site. An agent cannot make a component selection decision from them. No structured intent, no do/don't rules, no when-to-use guidance.

3.4_usage_guidance_formalisation.narrative: Code-only dimension. Not assessed in this Figma-primary audit.

3.5_documentation_frame_metadata.narrative: 6/6 page-level descriptions present (real text). 3/17 sub-component descriptions real (18%). Rich structural metadata: 941 variants, 38 variant axes, 19 boolean props, 10 text props, 7 instance swap props, 3 compound components. Structure is machine-readable; intent content is absent.

4.1_interaction_targets.narrative: Touch and click targets meet 44px minimum on most interactive components; a small number of icon-only actions fall below threshold.

4.2_contrast_ratios.narrative: Primary and secondary palette tokens meet WCAG AA contrast against their intended backgrounds; some lighter variants are borderline.

4.3_type_scale_consistency.narrative: Typography scale follows a consistent ratio across headings and body; one non-standard size step exists between subtitle and body.

4.4_type_completeness.narrative: Core type styles defined for headings, body, and captions; overline and subtitle2 lack full variable binding for lineHeight and letterSpacing.

4.5_spacing_scale_regularity.narrative: Spacing scale is perfectly regular with an 8px base unit: 1=8, 2=16, 3=24, through to 14=112.

4.6_grid_and_layout_system.narrative: Grid component exists with 12-column structure; breakpoint variables defined but layout tokens are not bound to a responsive system in Figma.

4.7_motion_duration_ranges.narrative: No motion duration tokens or variables present in the Figma file; transitions exist only in code.

4.8_motion_easing.narrative: No easing curves defined as variables or documented in the Figma file; code defines easeInOut, easeOut, and sharp curves.

4.9_focus_state_presence.narrative: Focus state variants present on 171 of 826 stateful components; coverage is strong on primary inputs and buttons but inconsistent on secondary interactive elements.

4.10_error_state_coverage.narrative: Error states present on form components (TextField, Select, Checkbox) with colour and helper text; missing on some secondary input components.

4.11_loading_state_coverage.narrative: CircularProgress and LinearProgress components exist; loading states not integrated as variants on Button, Card, or other content-bearing components.

4.12_empty_state_coverage.narrative: No empty state patterns or placeholder components found in the Figma file.

4.13_colour_system_structure.narrative: Full Material colour system with 252 primitive colour variables, 136 semantic palette variables, and complete alias chains from palette to primitives.

4.14_icon_sizing_consistency.narrative: Icons follow a consistent 24px default with small (20px) and large (35px) variants; sizing is not bound to variables.

4.15_elevation_shadow_system.narrative: 24 elevation levels defined as effect styles matching the Material elevation scale; not yet migrated to Variables.

4.16_visibility_of_system_status.narrative: Snackbar, Alert, CircularProgress, and LinearProgress components provide clear system status feedback patterns.

4.17_match_between_system_and_real_world.narrative: Component naming follows Material Design conventions which are well-established but not always intuitive (e.g. Fab, Chip).

4.18_user_control_and_freedom.narrative: Dialog and Drawer components support dismissal; undo patterns are not represented as components.

4.19_consistency_and_standards.narrative: Consistent application of Material Design 3 patterns across all component pages with uniform variant naming and state conventions.

4.20_error_prevention.narrative: Form validation states exist on TextField but confirmation dialogs and destructive action safeguards are not formalised as patterns.

4.21_recognition_rather_than_recall.narrative: Autocomplete, Select, and Menu components provide recognition-based interaction patterns with visible options.

4.22_flexibility_and_efficiency.narrative: Components support multiple sizes and density variants; keyboard shortcut patterns are not represented in the component library.

4.23_aesthetic_and_minimalist_design.narrative: Components follow Material Design's information density principles but some compound components carry visual complexity that may not be necessary in all contexts.

4.24_error_recovery.narrative: No error recovery patterns (retry, undo, rollback) represented as components or documented patterns in the Figma file.

4.25_help_and_documentation.narrative: No tooltip-based help, onboarding, or contextual documentation patterns present in the component library.

4.26_visual_hierarchy.narrative: Typography scale and elevation system support hierarchy; no explicit hierarchy guidelines or tokens for emphasis levels.

4.27_visual_rhythm_and_proportion.narrative: 8px base unit provides rhythmic spacing; proportional relationships between components are not formalised beyond the grid.

5.1_naming_convention_consistency.narrative: Tokens use slashes consistently. Components use angle brackets and variant=value syntax. Minor inconsistencies with internal prefixes (*Library, *Custom, _components, _fontSize).

5.2_versioning_and_changelog_discipline.narrative: Metadata variables record version (v7.2.0). Footer text on every heading shows version. No structured changelog in the Figma file.

5.3_contribution_standards.narrative: Code-only dimension. Not assessed in this Figma-primary audit.

5.4_deprecation_patterns.narrative: No visible deprecation markers on components or variables in the Figma file.

5.5_test_coverage.narrative: Code-only dimension. Not assessed in this Figma-primary audit.

5.6_adoption_visibility.narrative: Code-only dimension. Not assessed in this Figma-primary audit.

5.7_code_consistency_and_pattern_predict.narrative: Code-only dimension. Not assessed in this Figma-primary audit.

6.1_token_value_parity.narrative: 330 of 337 matched tokens have identical values (98%). 7 genuine mismatches: warning/main colour drift, 3 material/colors subtle drifts, h4 fontSize, fontFamily scope, breakpoints/xs semantics.

6.2_token_naming_parity.narrative: 100% naming alignment across all matched tokens.

6.3_component_naming_parity.narrative: Figma and code use identical base component names. Variant naming conventions differ syntactically (Figma: Size=Medium, code: size='medium') but are predictable.

6.4_variant_and_state_coverage_parity.narrative: Figma has comprehensive state variants matching code states. Button has 360 Figma variants across 4 axes matching code prop combinations. Not deeply cross-referenced at scale.

6.5_behaviour_parity.narrative: Not assessed. Requires deeper simultaneous inspection of Figma interactions and code behaviour.

6.6_documentation_of_parity_gaps.narrative: No documented parity gaps found. The 7 value mismatches and structural differences (spacing not in Variables, typography sub-properties missing) are undocumented.

findings.description: Elevation/shadow implemented as 24 effect styles rather than Figma Variables. Typography properties beyond fontSize (lineHeight, letterSpacing) stored as text style properties rather than variables.
findings.recommendation: Migrate elevation values to Variables when Figma supports effect variables. Document the text style / variable split.

findings.description: Spacing collection (14 variables) uses flat values with no alias chain. No semantic layer (e.g. spacing/compact, spacing/comfortable) aliasing to primitives.
findings.recommendation: Introduce a semantic spacing layer aliasing to the base-unit primitives.

findings.description: Three-layer architecture exists only for colour. Spacing, typography, shape, and elevation lack semantic and component layers. An agent working with spacing must use raw numeric values (1=8, 2=16) with no semantic context.
findings.recommendation: Add semantic spacing tokens (e.g. spacing/inset, spacing/stack, spacing/inline) aliasing to primitives. Add component-level spacing tokens per component.

findings.description: 84% of Figma Variables have no description. All 252 material/colors primitives, all 14 spacing variables, all 35 typography variables, and all 5 breakpoint variables lack descriptions.
findings.recommendation: Add descriptions to all token collections. Priority: spacing (no semantic names, so descriptions are the only context), typography (multiple overlapping size values), and primitives (no usage guidance).

findings.description: Spacing values hardcoded as px across all components checked via MCP. Alert uses px-[16px], py-[6px], pr-[12px], gap-[4px], p-[5px]. No spacing token binding detected.
findings.recommendation: Bind component spacing properties to spacing variables rather than hardcoded px values.

findings.description: 35.8% of components have any description. Of those, 96.2% are code import snippets (import TextField from '@mui/material/TextField'). Functional intent coverage is approximately 0%.
findings.recommendation: Replace code snippets with functional descriptions: when to use, when not to use, expected behaviour, constraints. Prioritise the 6 primary component pages.

findings.description: Zero components have structured intent documentation. The 14 non-code-snippet descriptions are one-sentence summaries insufficient for agent decision-making.
findings.recommendation: Write intent documentation for each component: functional purpose, usage constraints, variant selection guidance, accessibility considerations.

findings.description: All 6 component pages have real page-level descriptions from the *Library / Component Heading.
14 of 17 sub-component sections have placeholder lorem ipsum descriptions in *Library / Component Information. 18% real description coverage.
findings.recommendation: Replace placeholder text with functional sub-component descriptions.

findings.description: No deprecation markers found on any component or variable in the Figma file.
findings.recommendation: Implement deprecation markers using variable descriptions or a naming convention (e.g. [DEPRECATED] prefix).

findings.description: warning/main colour mismatch between Figma and code: Figma resolves to a different shade than the code default theme.
3 material/colors tokens show subtle value drift between Figma variables and code defaults.
h4 fontSize, fontFamily fallback scope, and breakpoints/xs semantics differ between Figma and code.
7 token value mismatches and multiple structural gaps (spacing not in Variables, typography sub-properties absent, shadows/zIndex/transitions code-only) are undocumented.
findings.recommendation: Create a parity gap register documenting known differences between Figma and code. Track which gaps are intentional (e.g. fontFamily fallback stack) and which are drift (e.g. warning/main colour).

data_gaps.description: MCP spot-checks limited to Alert component only. Button, TextField, Checkbox not spot-checked. Binding scores may not be representative.
Code-only dimensions (2.2, 2.4, 3.2, 3.4, 5.3, 5.5, 5.6, 5.7, 6.5) scored null. 9 of 44 dimensions not assessed.
Craft baseline Tier 1 dimensions 4.7 and 4.8 (motion) scored 0 because motion tokens are absent from Figma. Code has transition values that were not scored against.
